sanskrit:
न मे पार्थास्ति कर्तव्यं त्रिषु लोकेषु किञ्चन।
नानवाप्तमवाप्तव्यं वर्त एव च कर्मणि॥
transliteration:
na me pārthāsti kartavyaṃ triṣu lokeṣu kiñcana |
nānavāptam avāptavyaṃ varta eva ca karmaṇi ||
literal_meaning: O Pārtha, I have nothing that must be done in the three worlds, nothing unattained to be attained—yet I am engaged in action.
contextual_meaning: Krishna models self-driven action: even when no external demand or deficit exists, one can act from intrinsic purpose and care for others, dissolving the paralysis of 'What’s the point if I don’t have to?'
psychological_state: Arjuna shifts from anxious obligation toward an internalized, values-based work ethic; the ego’s scarcity narrative is challenged by an abundance model of action.
trigger_scenario: A high-achiever on sabbatical feels empty without deadlines; realizing that contribution can flow from fullness, they volunteer purely for joy and impact.
